requirements.title: Broker Root Structure
requirements.description: The broker root shall hold a pipe registry pointer, registry length, and registry capacity.
requirements.rationale: The broker root holds the pipe registry that enables publish/subscribe message routing, providing the core data needed to fan out messages to subscribers.

requirements.title: Broker API Interface
requirements.description: The broker API vtable shall define Publish and RegisterSubscriber operations.
requirements.rationale: Publish and RegisterSubscriber are the minimal operations needed for a publish/subscribe software bus: producers publish messages and consumers register pipes to receive them.

requirements.title: Broker Initialization
requirements.description: The broker shall initialize from a caller-provided pipe registry array and capacity.
requirements.rationale: Initialization from a caller-provided registry array ensures the broker operates without dynamic allocation and that the registry capacity is under integrator control.

requirements.title: Pipe Derivation
requirements.description: A pipe shall derive from the queue module root with an additional message ID field.
requirements.rationale: Deriving a pipe from the queue root reuses the existing FIFO implementation and adds only the message ID field needed for topic-based routing, minimizing code duplication.

requirements.title: Pipe Initialization
requirements.description: A pipe shall initialize with a message ID and a backing queue array.
requirements.rationale: Initializing a pipe with a message ID and backing queue establishes the subscription topic and buffer in a single step, with all memory caller-owned.

requirements.title: Publish Fan-Out
requirements.description: The broker shall enqueue a message into every registered pipe whose message ID matches the published message ID.
requirements.rationale: Fan-out delivery to all matching pipes is the defining behavior of a publish/subscribe bus, decoupling producers from consumers by message topic.

requirements.title: Publish Non-Matching Skip
requirements.description: The broker shall skip pipes whose message ID does not match the published message ID.
requirements.rationale: Skipping non-matching pipes ensures that subscribers only receive messages for their registered topic, preventing cross-contamination between message channels.

requirements.title: Register Subscriber
requirements.description: The broker shall append a pipe pointer to the registry on registration.
requirements.rationale: Appending a pipe to the registry is the mechanism by which consumers subscribe to the software bus, establishing the routing table for future publish operations.

requirements.title: Registry Full Rejection
requirements.description: The broker shall return an error status when registration is attempted on a full registry.
requirements.rationale: Explicit rejection on a full registry surfaces the capacity limit to the caller rather than silently failing to register a subscriber.

requirements.title: Broker Vtable Validation
requirements.description: The broker shall verify that its API vtable entries are non-null before each operation.
requirements.rationale: Validates the vtable and registry dependency at runtime to ensure memory safety before dispatching any broker operation.

requirements.title: Broker Registry Validation
requirements.description: The broker shall verify that its pipe registry pointer and capacity are valid before each operation.
requirements.rationale: Validates the registry pointer and capacity at runtime to catch uninitialized or misconfigured broker instances before they produce undefined behavior.

requirements.title: Pipe Validation
requirements.description: The broker shall verify each pipe via the queue verification before publishing to it.
requirements.rationale: Verifying each pipe before publishing to it prevents the broker from enqueuing messages into uninitialized or corrupted queue instances.

requirements.title: Single Thread Scope
requirements.description: The broker shall operate within a single thread without thread-safety mechanisms.
requirements.rationale: Single-thread scope avoids the complexity and overhead of synchronization primitives, which aligns with the deterministic, freestanding design philosophy; thread safety can be layered externally via the mutex module if needed.
